title: Intensely Sour Ethiopian Vegetables Atakilt Wat
description: A sour, silky, and show-off Ethiopian dish featuring vanilla and wood-fired bake, perfect for a diabetic-friendly meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the sliced carrots and shredded cabbage with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, and season with salt.
Spread the carrot and cabbage mixture on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat, and saute the chopped onions until they are translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the diced wood-fired baked bell peppers, vanilla extract, and lemon juice, and cook for 2 minutes.
Combine the roasted carrot and cabbage mixture with the skillet mixture, and season with salt to taste.
Serve the Intensely Sour Ethiopian Vegetables Atakilt Wat hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
